tutorMessages: Name the first constraint before proposing a solution.
Compare that constraint with the evidence you already have.
State the smallest defensible conclusion and one remaining doubt.
learnerMessages: I think the first constraint is limited evidence.
The evidence supports only a narrow conclusion.
I can defend the narrow claim while keeping one doubt open.

tutorMessages: Separate the observation from the explanation.
Test whether the explanation predicts the second observation.
Revise the explanation only where the prediction failed.
learnerMessages: The observation is clear but my explanation is tentative.
My explanation does not predict the second observation.
I changed one clause but the overall account is still uncertain.

tutorMessages: Write the claim in a form that could be disproved.
Identify which counterexample would matter most.
Explain why the surviving claim is weaker but more reliable.
learnerMessages: The claim could be disproved by a clear counterexample.
I am less sure which counterexample matters most.
The weaker claim feels harder to explain than the original.

tutorMessages: Choose which uncertainty you want us to investigate first.
Use your chosen uncertainty to test both of our assumptions.
Decide which conclusion you can now own without borrowing mine.
learnerMessages: I want to investigate whether the evidence is representative.
That uncertainty exposes an assumption in both accounts.
I can own the limited conclusion because I tested the assumption.

tutorMessages: Tell me which distinction in your account deserves to survive.
Challenge my reading using that distinction as your criterion.
Rebuild the account so the distinction does real explanatory work.
learnerMessages: The distinction between cause and correlation should survive.
Your reading collapses that distinction too quickly.
My revised account uses the distinction to explain the second case.

tutorMessages: Set the standard by which you want your claim judged.
Apply that standard to my objection and your original claim alike.
Give the conclusion in your own terms and name its exact limit.
learnerMessages: The standard should be explanatory reach without hidden exceptions.
That standard weakens my claim and also limits your objection.
My conclusion explains both cases but does not settle the third.
